name: Acral Lick Dermatitis
description: Acral lick dermatitis (also called lick granuloma) is a chronic skin lesion caused by repetitive licking at a single site, usually the distal limb. It forms a firm, thickened, hairless, often ulcerated plaque. Underlying causes include anxiety, orthopaedic pain, or allergies.
symptoms: Firm thickened plaque on wrist or ankle
Persistent licking at a single site
treatment_overview: Address underlying cause (pain, anxiety, allergy). Topical antiseptics, antibiotics for secondary infection, anti-anxiety drugs, and physical deterrents (bandaging, E-collar). Laser therapy and intralesional steroids can help chronic lesions.
prevention_tips: Address anxiety and boredom with enrichment
